name: xforge-policy-engine
description: Executable policy rules. Each policy is a JSONLogic-style expression evaluated at runtime against the request (actor, action, resource, context).

policies.name: fiscal-changes-require-reviewer
policies.description: Any modification to files under .xforge/knowledge/curated-operational/fiscal/** must be approved by a reviewer or higher role.
policies.reason: Fiscal changes affect Brazilian tax calculations; reviewer approval is mandatory.

policies.name: immutable-scoring-is-readonly
policies.description: The xfs scoring system (defined in .kilo/rules/immutable-scoring.md) cannot be modified by anyone except owner, and every modification requires audit.
policies.reason: xfs scoring is immutable; only owner can modify, with full audit.

policies.name: global-memory-promotion-needs-admin
policies.description: Promoting knowledge to enterprise-standard or altering global memory requires admin or higher.
policies.reason: Global memory and enterprise knowledge promotion are admin-level operations.

policies.name: breaking-change-needs-human-review
policies.description: Workflows that have 'breaking-change' or 'migration' in their name, or that touch shared APIs, require explicit human review approval.
policies.reason: Breaking changes and migrations need human review before execution.

policies.name: lgpd-fiscal-needs-premium-ai
policies.description: Operations involving LGPD (data subject rights) or fiscal calculation logic must not be processed by local-only or economy AI providers — premium (or human) is required.
policies.reason: LGPD and fiscal accuracy demand zero-tolerance — premium provider or human required.

evaluation: first-deny-wins; if a require-policy matches, action is allowed but flagged for the required reviewer
